content:
[MESSAGE FROM ANOTHER AGENT]
Sender:   Researcher (ID: agent-NewAgent-1773527895535)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Task / Request
Please create a report on the Iran war using the following facts: On 28 February 2026, Israel and the United States launched surprise airstrikes on multiple sites and cities across Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and numerous other Iranian officials [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war]. Iran responded with missile and drone strikes against Israel, US bases, and US-allied countries [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war]. President Donald Trump says US forces “obliterated” Iranian military targets on Kharg island southwest coast of Iran, and warned oil infrastructure there could be attacked next [Source: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/3/14/iran-war-live-pentagon-vows-to-ramp-up-us-military-campaign-against-iran]. The price of Brent crude oil spiked back over $100 as Iranian strikes hit ships in the regions waters [Source: https://apnews.com/live/iran-war-israel-trump-03-12-2026]. President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israel strikes attack targeting military and government sites, officials said [Source: https://abcnews.com/International/live-updates/iran-live-updates/?id=130893022]. Iranian officials have repeatedly threatened retaliation after what they describe as US attacks on the country’s oil and energy facilities [Source: https://www.cnn.com/world/live-news/iran-war-us-israel-trump-03-14-26]. Secretary Hegseth called Operation Epic Fury “straightforward.” But while the United States strikes the Iranian navy, nuclear program, and missiles, Israel and Trump eye regime change [Source: https://www.csis.org/programs/latest-analysis-war-iran].
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Context (Recent conversation from sender — read this to understand the full situation)
  [Msg 1/6 - Priority: LOW] [User]: hey
  [Msg 2/6 - Priority: LOW] [Researcher]: Hello!

  [Msg 3/6 - Priority: LOW] [User]: can you do web search
  [Msg 4/6 - Priority: LOW] [Researcher]: [TOOL: web_search(query="web search")]
  [Msg 5/6 - Priority: LOW] [User]: SYSTEM TOOL RESULT: Title: Web search
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_search
Snippet: A search engine is a software system that provides hyperlinks to web pages, and other relevant information on the Web in response to a user's query. The user enters a query in a web browser or a mobile app, and the search results are typically presented as a list of hyperlinks accompanied by textual summaries and images. Users also have the option of limiting a search to specific types of results, such as images, videos, or news.For a search provider, its engine is part of a distributed computing system that can encompass many data centers throughout the world. The speed and accuracy of an engine's response to a query are based on a complex system of indexing that is continuously updated by automated web crawlers. This can include data mining the files and databases stored on web servers, although some content is not accessible to crawlers.There have been many search engines since the dawn of the Web in the 1990s; however, Google Search became the dominant one in the 2000s and has remained so. As of May 2025, according to StatCounter, Google holds approximately 89–90 % of the worldwide search share, with competitors trailing far behind: Bing (~4 %), Yandex (~2.5 %), Yahoo! (~1.3 %), DuckDuckGo (~0.8 %), and Baidu (~0.7 %). Notably, this marks the first time in over a decade that Google's share has fallen below the 90% threshold. The business of websites improving their visibility in search results, known as marketing and optimization, has thus largely focused on Google.

---

Title: Online search
URL: https://grokipedia.com/page/online_search
Snippet: Online search, also known as web search, is the process of using specialized software systems called search engines to discover and retrieve information from the internet based on user-submitted queries. These engines operate by systematically crawl…

---

Title: What is Brave Search? | Brave
URL: https://brave.com/search
Snipp
  [Msg 6/6 - Priority: LOW] [Researcher]: Fact: A search engine is a software system that provides hyperlinks to web pages and other relevant information on the Web in response to a user's query [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_search].
Fact: Google holds approximately 89–90% of the worldwide search share [Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_search].
Fact: Online search, also known as web search, is the process of using specialized software systems called search engines to discover and retrieve information from the internet based on user-submitted queries [Source: https://grokipedia.com/page/online_search].
Fact: Brave Search offers AI-powered answers and results from an independent index with real privacy [Source: https://brave.com/search].
Fact: Startpage is a private search engine that does not track users or their search history [Source: https://www.startpage.com/en/].

──────────────────────────────────────────────────

SYSTEM TOOL RESULT: Report successfully generated: Iran_War_Report.md
